 {"id":"bd-1sw8","title":"ct_eq timing + truncation cast bugs: state_model, anti_entropy, deterministic_seed, fleet_quarantine, rollback_bundle","description":"ct_eq timing-attack fixes (10 files), truncation cast fix (1 file), domain separator fixes (2 files), fail-closed boundary fixes (8 files). Total: 28+ bugs fixed across 21 source files.","notes":"
2026-03-03 GoldLantern support slice:
- Updated deterministic_seed test loops to remove truncating casts (i as u8 -> u8::try_from(i).expect(...)) in crates/franken-node/src/encoding/deterministic_seed.rs.
- Reviewed fleet_quarantine.rs path for ct_eq/truncation issues; no obvious fix identified in current tree.
- Offloaded validation attempted (rch exec ... cargo check -p frankenengine-node --lib) but rch build 29737366419669044 became progress_stale and was canceled.

2026-03-03 SageBeacon support slice:
- Patched crates/franken-node/src/connector/rollback_bundle.rs to remove direct checksum-map equality in security-sensitive paths.
  - Added ct_eq_checksum_maps helper (deterministic key iteration + constant-time value compare via ct_eq).
  - Replaced direct map equality in StateSnapshot::diff and config-schema health check inside rollback flow.
- Added regression tests:
  - test_state_snapshot_diff_config_checksum_mismatch
  - test_ct_eq_checksum_maps_key_mismatch
- Offloaded validation:

 {"id":"bd-1ta","title":"[PLAN 10.13] FCP Deep-Mined Expansion Execution Track (9I)","description":"Section: 10.13 — FCP Deep-Mined Expansion Execution Track (9I)

Strategic Context:
Deep connector/provider contract track: lifecycle FSMs, conformance harnesses, fencing, sandboxing, revocation freshness, and protocol hardening.

Execution Requirements:
- Preserve all scoped capabilities from the canonical plan.
- Keep contracts self-contained so future contributors can execute without reopening the master plan.
- Require explicit testing strategy (unit + integration/e2e) and structured logging/telemetry for every child bead.
- Require artifact-backed validation for performance, security, and correctness claims.

Dependency Semantics:
- This epic is blocked by its child implementation beads.
- Cross-epic dependencies encode strategic sequencing and canonical ownership boundaries.

## Success Criteria
- All child beads for this section are completed with acceptance artifacts attached and no unresolved blockers.
- Section-level verification gate for comprehensive unit tests, integration/e2e workflows, and detailed structured logging evidence is green.
- Scope-to-plan traceability is explicit, with no feature loss versus the canonical plan section.

## Optimization Notes
- User-Outcome Lens: "[PLAN 10.13] FCP Deep-Mined Expansion Execution Track (9I)" must improve operator confidence, safety posture, and deterministic recovery behavior under both normal and adversarial conditions.
- Cross-Section Coordination: child beads must encode integration assumptions explicitly to avoid local optimizations that degrade system-wide correctness.
- Verification Non-Negotiable: completion requires reproducible unit/integration/E2E evidence and structured logs suitable for independent replay.
- Scope Protection: any simplification must preserve canonical-plan feature intent and be justified with explicit tradeoff  {"id":"bd-1tks","title":"Establish next actionable lane when backlog is empty (2026-03-02)","description":"Run AGENTS/README/codebase archaeology, register with MCP Agent Mail, sync roster, run BV robot triage and BR ready checks, offload quality gates via RCH, and either claim discovered actionable work or record no-action state with evidence.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-02T01:24:41.371456595Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-02T01:47:04.587526867Z","closed_at":"2026-03-02T01:47:04.587494537Z","close_reason":"Completed archaeology + coordination lane: no open/ready beads; Agent Mail roster synced; RCH-offloaded checks passed for frankenengine-node lib/clippy(no-deps)/fmt.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1tnu","title":"[10.20] Implement trust barrier primitives and policy wiring (behavioral sandbox escalation, composition firewall, verified-fork pinning, staged rollout fences).","description":"## Why This Exists

The immunization planner (bd-2fid) proposes barrier sets, but those barriers need concrete runtime primitives to enforce. This bead implements the trust barrier primitives and policy wiring layer -- the enforcement mechanisms that actually restrict, sandbox, or fence dependency-graph choke points at runtime.

Four categories of barrier primitives are required: behavioral sandbox escalation (tightening sandbox constraints on high-risk nodes), composition firewall (preventing transitive capability leakage across dependency boundaries), verified-fork pinning (locking dependencies to verified fork snapshots rather than upstream), and staged rollout fences (gating dependency updates through progressive deployment phases).

These primitives must be independently testable, composable (multiple barriers on the same node), and enforceable at designated choke points with deterministic override semantics and audit receipts. The policy engine wires barrier configurations from the immunization planner into runtime enforcement.

Within the 9N enhancement map, this bead provides the enforcement layer that makes DGIS recommendations actionable. Without it, barrier plans are advisory-only.

## What This Must Do

1. Implement behavioral sandbox escalation primitive: dynamically tighten sandbox constraints on nodes flagged as high-risk by the topology metrics engine.
2. Implement composition firewall primitive: prevent transitive capability leakage across dependency boundaries for flagged subgraphs.
3. Implement verified-fork pinning primitive: lock specific dependencies to verified fork snapshots with signature verification on each load.
4. Implement staged rollout fences primitive: gate dependency updates through progressive deployment phases with automatic rollback triggers.
5. Ensure all primitives are independently testable with isolated unit tests.
6. Ensure primitives are composable: multiple barriers can be applied to the same node with well-defined precedence rules.
7. Implement a policy engine that translates barrier plan configurations (from bd-2fid) into runtime enforcement actions at designated choke points.
8. Support deterministic overrides with audit receipts: operators can override barriers with signed justification that is logged for audit.
9. Emit audit receipts for every barrier enforcement action (applied, overridden, expired).

## Acceptance Criteria

- Barrier primitives are independently testable and composable; policy engine can enforce barrier sets at designated choke points with deterministic overrides and audit receipts.
- Each of the 4 barrier types (sandbox escalation, composition firewall, fork pinning, staged rollout) has independent test coverage.
- Composition of 2+ barriers on a single node produces deterministic combined enforcement behavior.
- Override mechanism requires signed justification and emits audit receipt.
- Audit receipts are structured, timestamped, and include the overriding principal identity.

## Testing & Logging Requirements

- Unit tests: each barrier primitive in isolation (sandbox escalation enforcement, firewall boundary detection, fork pin verification, rollout fence progression); composition behavior with multi-barrier scenarios; override mechanism with valid/invalid justifications.
- Integration tests: policy engine wiring from barrier plan to runtime enforcement; audit receipt generation and verification; multi-node barrier deployment scenarios.
- Structured logging: barrier enforcement events with stable codes (DGIS-BARRIER-001 through DGIS-BARRIER-NNN); override events with principal attribution; audit receipt emission; trace correlation IDs linking enforcement to barrier plans.
- Deterministic replay: barrier enforcement trace logs (JSONL) enable post-hoc audit and replay.

## Expected Artifacts

- `src/security/dgis/barrier_primitives.rs` -- barrier primitive implementations
- `tests/integration/dgis_barrier_enforcement.rs` -- enforcement test suite
- `artifacts/10.20/dgis_barrier_enforcement_trace.jsonl` -- sample enforcement trace
- `artifacts/section_10_20/bd-1tnu/verification_evidence.json` -- machine-readable CI/release gate evidence
- `artifacts/section_10_20/bd-1tnu/verification_summary.md` -- human-readable verification summary

## Dependencies

None (standalone primitive layer; consumed by bd-2fid immunization planner and bd-2wod quarantine orchestrator)","acceptance_criteria":"- Barrier primitives are independently testable and composable; policy engine can enforce barrier sets at designated choke points with deterministic overrides and audit receipts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:37:06.914671199Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-21T05:04:31.094672144Z","closed_at":"2026-02-21T05:04:31.094640876Z","close_reason":"Implemented all 4 trust barrier primitives (sandbox escalation, composition firewall, verified-fork pinning, staged rollout fences) with BarrierEngine, audit receipts, override mechanism, composition conflict detection, policy plan wiring, JSONL export. 24 Rust tests, 11 verification gate checks, 13 Python tests. All artifacts delivered.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-20","self-contained","test-obligations"]}

 {"id":"bd-1xao","title":"[13] Success criterion: impossible-by-default adoption","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 13

Task Objective:
Track and gate production-grade adoption of impossible-by-default capabilities.

Execution Requirements:
- Make this requirement machine-verifiable and release-gated where applicable.
- Keep behavior self-documenting so future contributors do not need to re-open the master plan.

Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for rule/contract logic and error conditions.
- Integration/E2E tests for workflow-level enforcement.
- Structured logs with stable codes and trace IDs.
- Reproducible artifacts that prove contract satisfaction.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Success and failure invariants for [13] Success criterion: impossible-by-default adoption are explicitly quantified and machine-verifiable.
- Determinism requirements for [13] Success criterion: impossible-by-default adoption are validated across repeated runs and adversarial perturbations.


Expected Artifacts:
- Machine-readable verification artifact with explicit canonical path under artifacts/section_13/bd-1xao/verification_evidence.json, suitable for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary with explicit canonical path under artifacts/section_13/bd-1xao/verification_summary.md, linking implementation intent to measured validation outcomes.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- The implementation must deliver the exact capability named in the title, with no scope dilution and no silent feature omission.
- Validation artifacts must include capability-specific thresholds, pass/fail criteria, and reproducible evidence bundles tied to this bead ID.
- Program/section verification gates must be able to consume this bead's outputs without manual interpretation.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[13] Success criterion: impossible-by-default adoption" is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[13] Success criterion: impossible-by-default adoption" prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","acceptance_criteria":"1. 'Impossible-by-default' capabilities are defined: operations that are blocked unless explicitly enabled with cryptographic authorization.
2. At minimum, these capabilities are impossible-by-default: (a) arbitrary file system access outside project root, (b) outbound network to non-allowlisted hosts, (c) spawning child processes without sandbox, (d) loading unsigned extensions, (e) disabling hardening profiles.
3. Each impossible-by-default capability requires explicit opt-in via signed capability token with expiry.
4. Attempting a blocked operation produces a clear, actionable error message (not a generic permission denied).
5. Adoption metric: >= 90% of production deployments run with all impossible-by-default capabilities enforced (measured via telemetry).
6. No impossible-by-default capability can be silently disabled; any disabling is logged and alerted.
7. Evidence: capability_enforcement_report.json listing each capability, enforcement status, and opt-in rate.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:39:34.668279352Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-20T23:37:02.590071518Z","closed_at":"2026-02-20T23:37:02.590043385Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-13","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1xao","depends_on_id":"bd-pga7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:43:25.443838167Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1xbc","title":"[10.17] Add deterministic time-travel runtime capture/replay for extension-host workflows.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.17 — Radical Expansion Execution Track (9K)

Why This Exists:
Radical expansion track for proof-carrying speculation, Bayesian adversary control, time-travel replay, ZK attestations, and claim compiler systems.

Task Objective:
Add deterministic time-travel runtime capture/replay for extension-host workflows.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Captured executions replay byte-for-byte equivalent control decisions under same seed/input; incident replay includes stepwise state navigation and divergence explanation.

Expected Artifacts:
- `docs/specs/time_travel_runtime.md`, `src/replay/time_travel_engine.rs`, `tests/lab/time_travel_replay_equivalence.rs`, `artifacts/10.17/time_travel_replay_report.json`.

- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_17/bd-1xbc/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_17/bd-1xbc/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.
Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.17] Add deterministic time-travel runtime capture/replay for extension-host workflows.", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.17] Add deterministic time-travel runtime capture/replay for extension-host workflows." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.17] Add deterministic time-travel runtime capture/replay for extension-host workflows." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.17] Add deterministic time-travel runtime capture/replay for extension-host workflows." is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.17] Add deterministic time-travel runtime capture/replay for extension-host workflows." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","acceptance_criteria":"- Captured executions replay byte-for-byte equivalent control decisions under same seed/input; incident replay includes stepwise state navigation and divergence explanation.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:37:03.099102362Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T05:29:57.447111352Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T05:29:57.447089121Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-17","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1xbc","depends_on_id":"bd-274s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:43:18.349690460Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1xbr.1","title":"bd-1xbr support: cap adversary_graph log with deterministic eviction","description":"Implement a bounded-capacity guard for AdversaryGraph log entries in crates/franken-node/src/security/adversary_graph.rs using deterministic oldest-first eviction. Add focused regression tests for default capacity, zero-capacity clamp-to-one behavior, and eviction ordering stability.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"DarkCave","created_at":"2026-03-06T02:22:36.151699080Z","created_by":"DarkCave","updated_at":"2026-03-06T02:36:36.811426110Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T02:36:36.811373041Z","close_reason":"Implemented bounded adversary_graph log capacity and tests; broad rch validation blocked by unrelated existing repo failures/timeouts","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1xbr.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1xbr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T02:22:36.151699080Z","created_by":"DarkCave","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":113,"issue_id":"bd-1xbr.1","author":"DarkCave","text":"Implemented bounded-capacity guard for AdversaryGraph log entries in crates/franken-node/src/security/adversary_graph.rs via DEFAULT_MAX_LOG_ENTRIES, with_log_capacity(...), log_capacity(), and deterministic oldest-first eviction through push_log_entry(...). Added regression tests for default capacity, zero-capacity clamp-to-one behavior, and oldest-first eviction ordering. Validation was offloaded via rch only: rch exec -- env RUSTUP_TOOLCHAIN=nightly rustfmt --check crates/franken-node/src/security/adversary_graph.rs exited 0 (warning only that rustfmt is a non-compilation command); broad cargo fmt --check remains noisy due unrelated shared-worktree formatting drift. Targeted rch exec -- env RUSTUP_TOOLCHAIN=nightly CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_darkcave_bd1xbr1 cargo test -p frankenengine-node adversary_graph --lib reached remote exit=101 in 311161ms on unrelated existing compile failures in connector/capability_guard.rs:739, conformance/fsqlite_inspired_suite.rs:402/412, control_plane/key_role_separation.rs:343/517, runtime/obligation_channel.rs:862, security/dgis/barrier_primitives.rs:632/769/825, and security/isolation_rail_router.rs:640; after that remote failure rch incorrectly attempted a local fallback, and I terminated only the exact fallback process tree immediately to preserve the offload-only requirement. Broad rch exec -- env RUSTUP_TOOLCHAIN=nightly CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_darkcave_bd1xbr1 cargo check -p frankenengine-node --all-targets exited 137 in 302202ms without new diagnostics. Broad rch exec -- env RUSTUP_TOOLCHAIN=nightly CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_darkcave_bd1xbr1 cargo clippy -p frankenengine-node --all-targets -- -D warnings exited 101 in 228689ms on unrelated existing type mismatches in security/dgis/barrier_primitives.rs:622/646/759. No diagnostics in these runs pointed at adversary_graph.rs.","created_at":"2026-03-06T02:36:36Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1xg","title":"[PLAN 10.4] Extension Ecosystem + Registry","description":"Section: 10.4 — Extension Ecosystem + Registry

Strategic Context:
Extension ecosystem and registry trust foundation: signed artifacts, provenance, trust cards, revocation, and quarantine flows.

Execution Requirements:
- Preserve all scoped capabilities from the canonical plan.
- Keep contracts self-contained so future contributors can execute without reopening the master plan.
- Require explicit testing strategy (unit + integration/e2e) and structured logging/telemetry for every child bead.
- Require artifact-backed validation for performance, security, and correctness claims.

Dependency Semantics:
- This epic is blocked by its child implementation beads.
- Cross-epic dependencies encode strategic sequencing and canonical ownership boundaries.

## Success Criteria
- All child beads for this section are completed with acceptance artifacts attached and no unresolved blockers.
- Section-level verification gate for comprehensive unit tests, integration/e2e workflows, and detailed structured logging evidence is green.
- Scope-to-plan traceability is explicit, with no feature loss versus the canonical plan section.

## Optimization Notes
- User-Outcome Lens: "[PLAN 10.4] Extension Ecosystem + Registry" must improve operator confidence, safety posture, and deterministic recovery behavior under both normal and adversarial conditions.
- Cross-Section Coordination: child beads must encode integration assumptions explicitly to avoid local optimizations that degrade system-wide correctness.
- Verification Non-Negotiable: completion requires reproducible unit/integration/E2E evidence and structured logs suitable for independent replay.
- Scope Protection: any simplification must preserve canonical-plan feature intent and be justified with explicit tradeoff documentation.","notes":"Acceptance Criteria alias (plan-space normalization, 2026-02-20):
- The `Success Criteria` section in this bead is the canonical acceptance contract for closure.
- Closure additionally requires linked unit/integration/E2E verification evidence and detailed structured logging artifacts from all child implementation
